frameworks.name: resource-rational evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Accumulation balances information gain against cognitive effort

frameworks.name: time-continuous Bayesian updating
frameworks.one_line: New dynamic Bayesian model with two efficiency parameters

frameworks.name: performance-effort trade-off
frameworks.one_line: Optimal balance between accuracy and cognitive resource cost

frameworks.name: primacy-recency in evidence integration
frameworks.one_line: Individual tendencies to weight early vs late samples

core_question: How does the precise temporal structure of evidence, including breaks, shape the dynamics of evidence accumulation?
key_assumptions: Observers perform Bayesian updating over a noisy stimulus sequence
Encoding and memory maintenance have bounded resource efficiencies
Accumulation dynamics can be modulated post-break
Optimal trade-offs between performance and effort govern integration
manipulations: Break duration in the evidence sequence
Position of the break within the sequence
Otherwise regular noisy stimulus sequences vs perturbed ones
Angular position inference task across subjects
key_findings: Subjects compensated for memory loss during breaks by enhancing post-break integration
Evidence integration and memory maintenance were dynamically upregulated after breaks
Model with two efficiency parameters captured rich accumulation behavior
Individual differences in primacy vs recency weighting were predicted
interpretations: Evidence accumulation is a non-stationary, dynamically controlled process
Accumulation optimally balances incoming information against cognitive effort
